    Emission-Line Diagnostics

    Emission-Line Diagnostics

    Type:Observational technique
    Level:Specialist
    Key lines:Hα, Hβ, [O III] λ5007, [N II] λ6584, [S II], [O I]
    Related: Spatially resolved stellar populations
    Star formation
    Metallicity gradients
    ISM

    Emission-line diagnostics use the ratios of nebular emission lines to classify the ionization source in galaxies and derive physical properties of the ionized gas. The most widely used tool is the BPT diagram (Baldwin, Phillips & Terlevich 1981[1]), which plots [O III]/Hβ against [N II]/Hα to separate star-forming regions, AGN, and composite/LINER ionization[1][2].

    BPT Diagram

    The BPT diagram separates ionization mechanisms because the harder radiation field of AGN produces stronger [O III] and [N II] relative to Balmer lines compared to HII regions ionized by young stars[1]. Additional diagnostic diagrams use [S II]/Hα and [O I]/Hα ratios. In retired galaxies (e.g., M51b), weak emission powered by hot low-mass evolved stars (HOLMES) falls in the LINER/retired region of the BPT diagram[2][3].

    Gas-Phase Metallicity from Emission Lines

    Strong-line calibrations use emission-line ratios to estimate nebular (gas-phase) oxygen abundance, 12 + log(O/H). Common indicators include O3N2 = log([O III]/Hβ / [N II]/Hα) and the N2 index[4]. These are used to construct metallicity gradient maps in spatially resolved studies[5][6].

    Hα as Star Formation Tracer

    Hα emission traces ionizing photons from massive O/B stars, making the Hα equivalent width (EW) a direct indicator of specific star formation rate. In the M51 group, spatially resolved Hα EW maps reveal star-forming spiral arms in M51a and retired regions in M51b, while M63 shows asymmetric Hα patterns consistent with outside-in quenching[6].

    See Also

    • Spatially resolved stellar populations
    • Metallicity gradients
    • Interstellar medium
    • Thainá-Batista et al. (2026) — Emission-line maps of M51 group

    References
    1. Baldwin, J. A., Phillips, M. M. & Terlevich, R. (1981). "Classification parameters for the emission-line spectra of extragalactic objects." PASP, 93, 5.
    2. Cid Fernandes, R. et al. (2011). "True vs. fake AGN." MNRAS, 413, 1687. DOI:10.1111/j.1365-2966.2011.18244.x
    3. Stasińska, G. et al. (2008). "Retired galaxies: not quiescent, but powered by old stars." MNRAS.
    4. Barrera-Ballesteros, J. K. et al. (2016). "Resolved metallicity relations." MNRAS. DOI:10.1093/mnras/stw1984
    5. Lacerda, E. A. D. et al. (2018). "Retired galaxies and DIG emission." MNRAS. DOI:10.1093/mnras/stx3022
    6. Thainá-Batista, J. et al. (2026). "Spatially resolved stellar populations in the M51 group with J-PLUS. II." A&A (accepted). arXiv:2603.15869
